text:

According to the navigation skills, when the goal is far to the right and I am far from the target, moving forward is still a reasonable action to make progress, even if it's not perfectly aligned. However, turning right would be more efficient to align with the goal direction.

Given the large bearing difference (+152 degrees), a single TURN_RIGHT (30 degrees) will not fully align me, but it will start correcting my heading. Since the distance is still large (1828 cm), I should prioritize making progress while adjusting my heading.

- But since the bearing is very large (+152), turning might be more effective to reduce the angular error.
